an2.118:0.2: 11. The Chapter on Desire That Are Hard to Give Up
an2.119:1.1: “Monks, these two people are hard to find in the world.
an2.119:1.3: The one who is first to do a kindness, and the one who is grateful for a kindness done and feels obligated to repay it.
an2.119:1.4: These two people are hard to find in the world.”
an2.121:1.1: “Monks, these two persons rarely have enough.
an2.121:1.3: One who piles [hoards] gain & gain, and one who spends [squanders] gain & gain.
an2.121:1.4: These two persons rarely have enough.”
an2.125:1.1: “Monks, there are these two conditions for the arising of wrong view.
an2.125:1.3: The voice of another and inappropriate attention.
an2.125:1.4: These are the two conditions for the arising of wrong view.”
an2.126:1.1: “Monks, there are these two conditions for the arising of right view.
an2.126:1.3: The voice of another and appropriate attention.
an2.126:1.4: These are the two conditions for the arising of right view.”
